encounter.type: Office visit, follow-up consultation — cardiac electrophysiology
encounter.reason: Holter monitor results review, catheter ablation candidacy discussion for persistent atrial fibrillation
encounter.service_provider: University of Minnesota Cardiac Electrophysiology Clinic
encounter.location: University of Minnesota Cardiac Electrophysiology Clinic, 420 Delaware Street SE, MMC 508, Minneapolis, MN 55455

conditions.name: persistent atrial fibrillation
conditions.note: Refined from atrial fibrillation to persistent atrial fibrillation based on 48-hour Holter monitor confirming 100% AF burden with no periods of sinus rhythm. Symptomatic despite adequate rate control. Decision made to pursue rhythm-control strategy via catheter ablation.

medications.name: metoprolol succinate 50 mg oral extended-release tablet

document_references.description: Detailed follow-up consultation note documenting review of 48-hour Holter monitor results confirming persistent atrial fibrillation with 100% AF burden. Includes comprehensive shared decision-making discussion with patient and wife regarding pulmonary vein isolation via catheter ablation, covering success rates, risks, alternatives, recovery expectations. Documents patient's decision to proceed with ablation and pre-procedure planning steps initiated.

diagnostic_reports.description: Pre-operative labs ordered in preparation for planned pulmonary vein isolation procedure. To be drawn prior to the procedure date.

diagnostic_reports.type: transesophageal echocardiogram or cardiac CT of left atrium
diagnostic_reports.description: Repeat TEE or cardiac CT ordered for left atrial anatomy mapping and thrombus exclusion prior to catheter ablation procedure. To be arranged before the procedure date.
